notes: Map-phase findings normalized to P0-P4 severity bands and chunk labels for reduce/council input.

findings.finding: LOW_WIN_RATE_THRESHOLD is referenced but not defined in creative_analyzer, causing NameError when low-win-rate checks run.

evidence.description: Module constants exist, but LOW_WIN_RATE_THRESHOLD is not defined.
LOW_WIN_RATE_THRESHOLD is used in SQL parameter binding.
LOW_WIN_RATE_THRESHOLD is used again for evidence threshold output.

impact.description: Recommendation generation can fail with NameError on paths that execute low-win-rate checks.

findings.finding: HIGH_WASTE_RATE_THRESHOLD is referenced but not defined in geo_analyzer, causing NameError in high-waste geo checks.

evidence.description: Threshold constants are defined, but HIGH_WASTE_RATE_THRESHOLD is absent.
HIGH_WASTE_RATE_THRESHOLD is used in conditional logic.
HIGH_WASTE_RATE_THRESHOLD is used for emitted evidence threshold.

impact.description: Geo recommendation paths can crash when high-waste checks execute.

findings.finding: selectedBuyerId is missing from the qps-summary query key, allowing cross-buyer cache contamination.

evidence.description: Query key includes only ["qps-summary", days].
selectedBuyerId is passed to the API call but not reflected in query key.
Another query (rtb-funnel) includes selectedBuyerId correctly, confirming inconsistency.

impact.description: Users can see stale QPS data from a previously selected buyer account.

findings.finding: Waste percentage is defined using inconsistent funnel stages across analytics modules, producing contradictory numbers.

evidence.description: Waste is derived from 100 - win_rate where win_rate is based on reached_queries.
Waste is computed as (bid_requests - auctions_won) / bid_requests.
Win rate denominator uses reached_queries.

impact.description: Different pages can report materially different waste rates for the same account and period.

findings.finding: Critical home-page analytics queries disable retries (retry: 0), increasing hard-failure frequency on transient errors.

evidence.description: pretargeting-configs query sets retry: 0.
rtb-funnel-configs query sets retry: 0.

impact.description: A single timeout can leave key dashboard sections in failed state until manual refresh.

findings.finding: Dashboard API default timeout is 15s, which is tight for analytics queries on larger datasets.

evidence.description: DEFAULT_API_TIMEOUT_MS is set to 15000.
Timeout path aborts request and throws timeout error.

impact.description: Slow but valid analytics responses can fail client-side, increasing error rates.

findings.finding: Seat selection initialization depends on async seats fetch and may temporarily gate all analytics queries.

evidence.description: Seats are loaded asynchronously via useQuery.
selectedBuyerId is auto-set only after seats resolve.
All key queries are gated by seatReady.

impact.description: Slow seat initialization can leave users in a non-data state longer than expected and amplify perceived load failures.

findings.finding: Config metrics fallback to zeros when performance payload is absent; warning banner exists but values can still be misread as real zero performance.

evidence.description: transformConfigToProps defaults reached/impressions/win_rate/waste_rate to 0.
UI shows delayed-metrics banner when configPerformanceLoading or configPerformanceError is true.

impact.description: Users may treat temporary fallback zeros as actual campaign performance despite warning copy.

findings.finding: Savings estimates rely on a hardcoded CPM constant ($0.002 per 1000) rather than account-specific spend data.

evidence.description: ESTIMATED_COST_PER_1000 constant is hardcoded to 0.002.
Monthly savings derive directly from ESTIMATED_COST_PER_1000.
Geo analyzer uses inline 0.002-based savings estimate.

impact.description: Savings can be materially under/overstated relative to true account CPM.

findings.finding: QPS estimate is average-only (daily_requests / 86400), which can understate peak operational load.

evidence.description: SECONDS_PER_DAY is defined as 86400.
daily_requests is averaged across the selected day count.
estimated_qps uses average daily_requests / SECONDS_PER_DAY.

impact.description: Capacity planning signals can be lower than peak-hour reality.

findings.finding: Importer pipeline does not canonicalize size strings with utils/size_normalization.canonical_size() before persistence.

evidence.description: canonical_size() helper exists for normalization.
No canonical_size() usage found in importer code paths.
Analyzer imports and relies on canonical size normalization utilities.

impact.description: Inconsistent raw size formats can reduce match quality in analytics and recommendation joins.

findings.finding: Fraud analyzer uses a single fixed suspicious CTR threshold (10%) without format-aware calibration.

evidence.description: SUSPICIOUSLY_HIGH_CTR is fixed at 0.10.
Fixed threshold drives suspicion checks directly.

impact.description: Can increase false positives/negatives across different creative formats and traffic profiles.

findings.finding: Geo-mismatch functionality is partially wired: preview modal calls the endpoint, but mismatch context is not surfaced in the main creative card/list view.

evidence.description: Backend geo-mismatch method is implemented.
Preview modal fetches geo mismatch via getCreativeGeoMismatch().
Creative card renders language label but no mismatch alert surface.

impact.description: Mismatch signals exist but are not consistently visible at high-level browsing surfaces.

findings.finding: Currency mismatch feature is not implemented: creative model lacks currency_code and no country-currency mismatch logic exists.

evidence.description: Creative model fields include language metadata but no currency_code field.
No currency-related creative fields are defined in the model.

impact.description: Users cannot detect or act on currency-country targeting mismatches.

findings.finding: Recommendation cards expose resolve/dismiss only and do not execute actionable API operations directly.

evidence.description: UI action buttons are Mark Resolved and Dismiss; no Apply action wiring.
Recommendation API supports resolve endpoint.
Separate settings API provides apply endpoint for pretargeting actions.

impact.description: Optimization workflow remains mostly manual despite actionable recommendation payloads.

findings.finding: Home dashboard lacks a prominent global 'Data as of' timestamp for key metrics.

evidence.description: No explicit 'data as of' timestamp is rendered on the main home dashboard.
Stale-cache indicator exists at creative-card level, but not as a home-level data freshness banner.

impact.description: Users may not know recency of aggregate analytics before acting on recommendations.

findings.finding: Campaign sort/filter state is local component state and is not persisted to URL, so navigation can reset analyst context.

evidence.description: Page-level sort/filter state is held in useState.
countryFilter is local state.
showIssuesOnly is local state.

impact.description: Analysts can lose current filter/sort context on navigation and need to reapply controls.

findings.finding: Import flow records min/max date ranges but does not enforce continuity checks for missing expected report days.

evidence.description: Importer stores date_range_start.
Importer stores date_range_end.
No continuity/gap check found for expected daily date coverage.

impact.description: Missing daily input files can create silent analytics gaps with no explicit alerting.
